originalTitle: Manhunt under way in South Africa after 12 killed in mass shooting in Johannesburg
title: Grown-ups Are Helping After Sad Day in Johannesburg!
content:
Hello from Your Friendly News Reporter!

Hello, amazing kids of Africa! Your friendly news reporter is here. We live on a big, beautiful continent filled with wonderful people and places. Sometimes, grown-ups have to share news that makes us feel a little sad. Today, we have some sad news from a very big city in South Africa called Johannesburg. It's a place where many families live, just like in your town. But don't worry, lots of grown-ups are working very, very hard to make sure everyone is safe and to help people who are sad or hurt. They are like superheroes, always trying to make things better. We always want everyone to feel safe, happy, and loved. Let's learn about what happened and how people are helping.

What Happened in Johannesburg?

Something very sad happened in Johannesburg. Some bad people caused trouble in a place where many families live. This place is like a small village, and people build their homes close together. Sadly, because of the trouble, some people got very, very hurt. Twelve people lost their lives, and nine other people got injured. This means they were hurt and needed doctors to help them get better. It happened late at night when most people were sleeping. It's important to remember that this kind of bad thing does not happen often, and many grown-ups are making sure it doesn't happen again.

The Police Are Busy Helping!

When something bad happens, like what happened in Johannesburg, the police become very busy. Police officers are like brave protectors for our communities. Right now, the police in South Africa are working super hard. They are looking for the bad people who caused the trouble. This is sometimes called a 'manhunt,' but it just means police are searching for the people who did wrong so they can make things right. They want to find these people so they can't hurt anyone else. The police are talking to everyone they can, looking for clues, and making sure justice is done. Their job is to keep all of us safe. Did you know? Police officers train very hard to be strong and smart. They learn how to solve puzzles and find clues, just like detectives in a storybook!

What is an Informal Settlement?

The place where the sad event happened is called an "informal settlement." This is a special kind of neighborhood where many families live together. Sometimes, people build their homes quickly with different materials. These places are often filled with strong communities where people help each other every day. Families live there, children play there, and grown-ups work hard. It's a part of the big city, just like any other neighborhood, but sometimes it doesn't have all the big buildings or roads that other parts of the city have. Even in these places, people look out for each other, and they want to live in peace. When something bad happens, it makes the community very sad, but it also makes them stronger in helping each other.

How Do People Help Each Other?

When sad things happen, people in the community always come together. They help their neighbors and friends. If someone is hurt, others bring them food or help them go to the doctor. Grown-ups who work for the government and special helper groups also come to informal settlements. They make sure people have what they need, like food, blankets, and help for anyone feeling sad or scared. It’s like when your friend needs a helping hand, and you give it to them. Everyone works together to make things better and to heal the community. Did you know? In many African cultures, people believe in 'Ubuntu,' which means 'I am because we are.' It means we are all connected and should always help each other!

Keeping Everyone Safe

It's very important for all of us to feel safe. Grown-ups, like your parents, teachers, and community leaders, are always working to keep you safe. If you ever feel scared or see something that doesn't look right, it's very important to tell a trusted grown-up right away. This could be your mom, dad, grandparent, teacher, or any adult you trust. They know what to do and how to help. Remember, you are never alone. There are always people who care about you and want to protect you. Did you know? South Africa is famous for its beautiful wildlife, like lions and elephants! But it's also famous for its kind people who love to help each other.

Looking to the Future

Even when sad things happen, we must always look to the future with hope. The police will keep working until they find the bad people. The community in Johannesburg will keep helping each other. And all of us, no matter where we live in Africa, can remember to be kind and helpful to others. Let's all hope for peace and safety for everyone in Johannesburg and all over Africa. Remember, you are important, and you are loved! Your friendly reporter, reminding you to always be kind and safe!
summary: Police in South Africa are working hard to find bad people who caused trouble in Johannesburg, where 12 people sadly lost their lives and 9 others were hurt, while the community and grown-ups are helping everyone stay safe.
whyMatters: This news matters because it shows that when sad things happen, police and communities work together to keep everyone safe and help people who are hurt, reminding us all to be kind and look out for each other.

originalTitle: World Cup referee denied US entry returns to Somalia to hero's welcome
title: Our African Hero Referee Comes Home to Cheers!
content:
Hey everyone, it's your friendly kid-reporter from Africa, bringing you some super exciting news! Today, we're talking about a very special person from our amazing continent, a brave football referee named Omar Artan. He comes from a beautiful country called Somalia, right here in Africa! Omar is a hero, and guess what? He just got the biggest, happiest welcome home ever!

Hello, Super Referee Omar!

Imagine a football game, with players running super fast and kicking the ball. Who makes sure everyone plays fair and follows the rules? That's right, it's the referee! Our friend Omar Artan is one of the best referees in the world. He's super good at his job, making sure every game is fair and fun for everyone. He wears a special uniform and has a whistle that he blows to stop the game or start it again. Referees are very important because they help keep the game safe and exciting for all. Did you know? Football, which some people call soccer, is the most popular sport in Africa! We love to play it and watch it with our friends and family. Maybe you even play football with your friends!

A Big Trip to Far-Away America

Omar Artan was invited to go on a very long journey. He was going to a country called the United States of America, far, far away across the big ocean! He was probably very excited because it's a big honour for a referee to travel and work in other countries. He must have packed his special referee uniform, his whistle, and maybe even some snacks for the long trip. Going to a new country is like a grand adventure!

A Little Problem on the Way

But something a little confusing happened when Omar got to America. Some grown-up people there, called 'officials,' said he couldn't come into their country. They said it was because they thought he might know some other people who were suspected of doing things that are not good, even though Omar himself is a very good person who always follows rules and loves fair play. It was a big surprise, and probably made Omar feel a little sad and confused because he's a kind and honest referee. It's important to remember that Omar is a good person who always wants fair play, both on and off the field. Sometimes, grown-ups make decisions that can be a bit hard to understand. But the most important thing is that Omar is a hero in his heart and in his country.

Coming Home Like a Star!

So, Omar couldn't go into America, and he had to turn around and come all the way back home to Somalia. But guess what? Instead of being sad, something magical happened! When his aeroplane landed back in Somalia, a huge crowd of people was waiting for him! They weren't angry; they were incredibly happy! There were people everywhere! They were cheering, clapping, singing, and waving flags. It was like a giant party! They were so excited to see Omar. Everyone wanted to shake his hand and tell him how proud they were of him. It was a hero's welcome, just like when a brave leader or a famous sports star comes home after doing something amazing. People were dancing in the streets, celebrating their hero from Somalia! Did you know? Somalia is a beautiful country in East Africa, known for its long coastline along the Indian Ocean! It's also famous for its delicious food and friendly people.

Why Was Everyone So Happy?

Why did everyone make such a big fuss for Omar? Because he is a true hero! Even though he had a little problem and couldn't go to America, his community still loves and supports him very much. They know he is a good person and a wonderful referee. They wanted to show him that no matter what happens, his home and his people will always welcome him with open arms and big smiles. It shows how strong our African communities are, always supporting each other! This kind of welcome is called a 'hero's welcome' because it makes someone feel very special and loved, showing them that their community believes in them. It's like when you score a winning goal, and all your friends cheer for you, but even bigger!

What Can We Learn from Omar?

Omar's story teaches us many important things. It shows us that it's okay if things don't always go as planned. What really matters is how you act, how kind you are, and how much your community loves you. Omar always plays fair, and that's why his people are so proud. He teaches us to be brave, to keep doing our best, and to always remember where we come from. He is an example of an African person who works hard and makes his country proud. Did you know? A true hero isn't just someone who saves the day, but also someone who shows courage, kindness, and never gives up, even when things are tough!

Our African Heroes!

Omar Artan is a shining star from Somalia, and a hero for all of Africa. His story reminds us that our continent is full of amazing people who do incredible things every day. We should always be proud of our brothers and sisters across Africa, whether they are football referees, teachers, doctors, or even talented young artists like some of you! Let's all cheer for Omar and for all the wonderful people who make Africa shine so brightly! What a fantastic story of community love and support!

originalTitle: Who is the Somali referee barred from entering the US for the World Cup?
title: Who is the Somali referee barred from entering the US for the World Cup?
content: Omar Artan was set to become the first Somali to referee a game at the World Cup finals, but his place in history has been denied by US immigration authorities.

title: Canada introduces bill to ban social media for children under 16
content: The bill also aims to make AI chatbots safer by establishing a digital regulator to set safety standards.

content: Portugal beat Nigeria 2-1 as Pedro Neto and Francisco Conceicao net, but Cristiano Ronaldo scuffs golden chances.

title: Trump says US will hit Iran 'hard' again today
content: The US president warns Iran "will have to pay the price" for taking too long to agree a deal, after Tehran vows retaliation to any attacks.

title: Three Indian sailors missing after US says it hit tanker in Gulf of Oman
content: India's government said three Indian sailors were missing and 21 crew had been rescued after the hit on the Settebello off the coast of Oman.

originalTitle: Fireworks illuminate Barcelona's Sagrada Família during Pope visit
title: Fireworks illuminate Barcelona's Sagrada Família during Pope visit
content: Pope Leo XIV says Barcelona's iconic Sagrada Família is a masterpiece of "stones, colours and light" during his visit to Spain.

originalTitle: Infantino could have stood up for the World Cup - but he said 'chill, relax'
title: Infantino could have stood up for the World Cup - but he said 'chill, relax'
content: Gianni Infantino faced the media for the first time in three years, but he did little to convince that Fifa has lost its grip on the organisation of the 2026 World Cup.

originalTitle: Legend of the club - but McKenna leaves promoted Ipswich
title: Legend of the club - but McKenna leaves promoted Ipswich
content: Ipswich Town announce that Kieran McKenna has decided to resign, before the club's Premier League campaign.

originalTitle: Is England v Costa Rica delay a sign of things to come?
title: Is England v Costa Rica delay a sign of things to come?
content: After England's game against Costa Rica was delayed by extreme weather, was that just a sign of things to come in the World Cup?
